preamble: In certain Nahuatl dialects, the lateral affricate [tɬ] has shifted to a velar stop [k]. This shift is part of a broader pattern of delateralization and backing seen in some Uto-Aztecan varieties.

examples.note: Shift seen in some modern northern dialects where 'tl' corresponds to 'k'.

sources: Canger, U. (1980). Five Hundred Years of Nahuatl Dialectology.
